 `./README.md`

```text
1	# MultiBench
2	
3	**Standardized toolkit for multimodal deep learning research**
4	
5	[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/multibench/badge/?version=latest)](https://multibench.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)
6	
7	![Overview](images/overview.png)
8	
9	MultiBench is a systematic, unified large-scale benchmark spanning **15 datasets**, **10 modalities**, **20 prediction tasks**, and **6 research areas**. It provides an automated end-to-end ML pipeline that simplifies data loading, experimental setup, and model evaluation.
10	
11	Paired with **MultiZoo**, a modular collection of 20 core multimodal learning approaches, MultiBench holistically evaluates:
12	
13	1. **Performance** across domains and modalities
14	2. **Complexity** during training and inference
15	3. **Robustness** to noisy and missing modalities
16	
17	 [Documentation](https://human-ai-lab.github.io/multibench/) · 
18	
19	## Supported datasets
20	
21	| Research Area        | Datasets                                           |
22	| -------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
23	| Affective Computing  | CMU-MOSI, CMU-MOSEI, MUStARD, UR-FUNNY            |
24	| Healthcare           | MIMIC                                              |
25	| Robotics             | Vision & Touch, MuJoCo Push (Gentle Push)          |
26	| Finance              | Stocks-Food, Stocks-Health, Stocks-Tech            |
27	| HCI                  | ENRICO                                             |
28	| Multimedia           | AV-MNIST, MM-IMDb, Kinetics-S, Kinetics-L         |
29	
30	![Datasets](images/datasets.png)
31	
32	## Supported algorithms
33	
34	![MultiZoo](images/multizoo.png)
35	
36	- **Unimodal models** — MLP, GRU, LSTM, CNN, LeNet, ResNet, Transformer, FCN, Random Forest, etc. (`unimodals/`)
37	- **Fusion paradigms** — Early/Late Fusion, Tensor Fusion, Multiplicative Interactions, Low-Rank Tensor Fusion, NL-Gate, MulT, etc. (`fusions/`)
38	- **Objective functions** — CrossEntropy, MSE, ELBO, CCA, Contrastive Loss, Reconstruction Loss, etc. (`objective_functions/`)
39	- **Training structures** — Supervised Learning (Early/Late Fusion, MVAE, MFM), Gradient Blend, Architecture Search, MCTN, etc. (`training_structures/`)
40	
41	## Getting started
42	
43	### Prerequisites
44	
45	- Python >= 3.10
46	- PyTorch (CPU or GPU)
47	
48	### Virtual environment  
49	It is advised to use a virtual environment to manage dependencies. You can create one using `uv venv` (recommended) or `venv`:
50	
51	```bash
52	uv venv
53	source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows, use `.venv\Scripts\activate`
54	```
55	If not using uv, you can create a virtual environment using `venv`:
56	
57	```bash
58	python -m venv .venv
59	source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows, use `.venv\Scripts\activate`
60	```
61	And remove `uv` from the installation commands below.
62	
63	### Installation
64	For GPU support, install using the appropriate command from the [PyTorch website](https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/). The following command installs the latest stable version of PyTorch with CUDA latest support:
65	
66	```bash
67	uv pip install -r requirements.txt
68	```
69	
70	For CPU, install directly:
71	
72	```bash
73	uv pip install torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu
74	uv pip install memory-profiler scikit-learn scipy matplotlib h5py tqdm
75	```
76	
77	### Quick example
78	
79	This example trains a simple early fusion model on the [CMU-MOSI](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1uEK737LXB9jAlf9kyqRs6B9N6cDncodq?usp=sharing) sentiment dataset.
80	
81	```bash
82	# Download the MOSI dataset
83	pip install gdown
84	gdown https://drive.google.com/u/0/uc?id=1szKIqO0t3Be_W91xvf6aYmsVVUa7wDHU
85	mkdir -p data/affect && mv mosi_raw.pkl data/affect/
86	```
87	
88	```python
89	import torch
90	from datasets.affect.get_data import get_dataloader
91	from unimodals.common_models import GRU, MLP, Sequential, Identity
92	from fusions.common_fusions import ConcatEarly
93	from training_structures.Supervised_Learning import train, test
94	
95	device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
96	
97	# Load data (3 modalities: text, audio, vision)
98	traindata, validdata, testdata = get_dataloader(
99	    'data/affect/mosi_raw.pkl',
100	    data_type='mosi', max_pad=True, max_seq_len=50
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-24 11:21:30Z)**_

No, users don't need to install multibench as a package. The README only instructs users to:

1. Clone the repo
2. Install dependencies (`pip install -r requirements.txt` or the individual packages)
3. Run scripts from within the project directory

The internal modules (`fusions`, `unimodals`, `datasets`, etc.) are imported directly from the local directory structure — there's no `pip install -e .` step in the docs.

That said, the `pyproject.toml` does define it as a proper package, so `pip install -e .` would also work if someone wanted it installed into their environment. But it's not required — just the dependencies are enough.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-24 11:30:15Z)**_

That tells the real story. Every example manually does `sys.path.insert(0, repo_root)` to add the repo root to the Python path. The tests do `sys.path.append(os.getcwd())` for the same reason.

**So no, multibench does not need to be installed.** Users only need:
1. The required packages installed (`torch`, `torchvision`, `memory-profiler`, `scikit-learn`, `scipy`, `matplotlib`, `h5py`, `tqdm`)
2. To either run from the repo root, or have scripts manually add the repo root to `sys.path` (as all examples do)

The `pyproject.toml` exists but is essentially unused in practice — the code works purely by path manipulation, not package installation.

1	"""Implements MVAE."""
2	import torch
3	from torch import nn
4	
5	
6	class ProductOfExperts(nn.Module):
7	    """
8	    Return parameters for product of independent experts.
9	
10	    See https://arxiv.org/pdf/1410.7827.pdf for equations.
11	    """
12	
13	    def __init__(self, size):
14	        """Initialize Product of Experts Object.
15	
16	        Args:
17	            size (tuple): Size of Product of Experts Layer
18	
19	        """
20	        super(ProductOfExperts, self).__init__()
21	        self.size = size
22	
23	    def forward(self, mus, logvars, eps=1e-8):
24	        """Apply Product of Experts Layer.
25	
26	        Args:
27	            mus (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Mus
28	            logvars (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Logvars
29	            eps (float, optional): Epsilon for log-exponent trick. Defaults to 1e-8.
30	
31	        Returns:
32	            torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor: Output of PoE layer.
33	        """
34	        device = mus[0].device
35	        mu, logvar = _prior_expert(self.size, len(mus[0]), device)
36	        mu = torch.cat([mu] + [m.unsqueeze(0) for m in mus], dim=0)
37	        logvar = torch.cat([logvar] + [lv.unsqueeze(0) for lv in logvars], dim=0)
38	
39	        var = torch.exp(logvar) + eps
40	        # precision of i-th Gaussian expert at point x
41	        T = 1. / var
42	        pd_mu = torch.sum(mu * T, dim=0) / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
43	        pd_var = 1. / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
44	        pd_logvar = torch.log(pd_var)
45	        return pd_mu, pd_logvar
46	
47	
48	class ProductOfExperts_Zipped(nn.Module):
49	    """
50	    Return parameters for product of independent experts.
51	    
52	    See https://arxiv.org/pdf/1410.7827.pdf for equations.
53	    """
54	
55	    def __init__(self, size):
56	        """Initialize Product of Experts Object.
57	
58	        Args:
59	            size (tuple): Size of Product of Experts Layer
60	        
61	        """
62	        super(ProductOfExperts_Zipped, self).__init__()
63	        self.size = size
64	
65	    def forward(self, zipped, eps=1e-8):
66	        """Apply Product of Experts Layer.
67	
68	        Args:
69	            mus (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Mus
70	            logvars (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Logvars
71	            eps (float, optional): Epsilon for log-exponent trick. Defaults to 1e-8.
72	
73	        Returns:
74	            torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor: Output of PoE layer.
75	        """
76	        mus = [i[0] for i in zipped]
77	        logvars = [i[1] for i in zipped]
78	        device = mus[0].device
79	        mu, logvar = _prior_expert(self.size, len(mus[0]), device)
80	        mu = torch.cat([mu] + [m.unsqueeze(0) for m in mus], dim=0)
81	        logvar = torch.cat([logvar] + [lv.unsqueeze(0) for lv in logvars], dim=0)
82	
83	        var = torch.exp(logvar) + eps
84	        T = 1. / var
85	        pd_mu = torch.sum(mu * T, dim=0) / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
86	        pd_var = 1. / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
87	        pd_logvar = torch.log(pd_var)
88	        return pd_mu, pd_logvar
89	
90	
91	def _prior_expert(size, batch_size, device):
92	    """Universal prior expert — spherical Gaussian N(0, 1)."""
93	    size = (size[0], batch_size, size[2])
94	    mu = torch.zeros(size, device=device)
95	    logvar = torch.zeros(size, device=device)  # log(1) == 0
96	    return mu, logvar
97
```

1	"""Implements MCTN for Fusions."""
2	
3	import random
4	import math
5	from torch.nn import functional as F
6	from torch import nn
7	import torch
8	import sys
9	import os

11	from torch.serialization import save

13	sys.path.append(os.getcwd())

17	class Encoder(nn.Module):
18	    """
19	    Apply a gated GRU to encode the input vector.
20	    
21	    Paper/Code Sourced From: https://arxiv.org/pdf/1812.07809.pdf.
22	    """
23	
24	    def __init__(self, input_size, hidden_size,
25	                 n_layers=1, dropout=0.2):
26	        """
27	        Create Encoder.
28	
29	        :param input_size: Encoder input size
30	        :param hidden_size: Hidden state size for internal GRU
31	        :param n_layers: Number of layers in recurrent unit
32	        :param dropout: Dropout Probability
33	        """
34	        super(Encoder, self).__init__()
35	        self.input_size = input_size
36	        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
37	
38	        self.gru = nn.GRU(input_size, hidden_size, n_layers,
39	                          dropout=dropout, bidirectional=True)
40	
41	    def forward(self, src, hidden=None):
42	        """
43	        Apply Encoder to input.
44	
45	        :param src: Encoder Input
46	        :param hidden: Encoder Hidden State
47	        """
48	        outputs, hidden = self.gru(src, hidden)
49	
50	        # sum bidirectional outputs
51	        outputs = (outputs[:, :, :self.hidden_size] +
52	                   outputs[:, :, self.hidden_size:])
53	        return outputs, hidden
54	
55	
56	class Attention(nn.Module):
57	    """Implements Attention Mechanism for MCTN."""
58	
59	    def __init__(self, hidden_size):
60	        """Initialize Attention Mechanism for MCTN.
61	
62	        Args:
63	            hidden_size (int): Hidden Size of Attention Layer.
64	        
65	        """
66	        super(Attention, self).__init__()
67	        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
68	        self.attn = nn.Linear(self.hidden_size * 2, hidden_size)
69	        self.v = nn.Parameter(torch.rand(hidden_size))
70	        stdv = 1. / math.sqrt(self.v.size(0))
71	        self.v.data.uniform_(-stdv, stdv)
72	
73	    def forward(self, hidden, encoder_outputs):
74	        """Apply Attention to Input, with Hidden Layers.
75	
76	        Args:
77	            hidden : Initial hidden state.
78	            encoder_outputs : Outputs of Encoder object.
79	
80	        Returns:
81	            output: Output of Attention layer
82	
83	        """
84	        timestep = encoder_outputs.size(0)
85	        h = hidden.repeat(timestep, 1, 1).transpose(0, 1)
86	        encoder_outputs = encoder_outputs.transpose(0, 1)  # [B*T*H]
87	        attn_energies = self._score(h, encoder_outputs)
88	        return F.relu(attn_energies).unsqueeze(1)
89	
90	    def _score(self, hidden, encoder_outputs):
91	        energy = F.softmax(
92	            self.attn(torch.cat([hidden, encoder_outputs], 2)), dim=1)
93	        energy = energy.transpose(1, 2)  # [B*H*T]
94	        v = self.v.repeat(encoder_outputs.size(0), 1).unsqueeze(1)  # [B*1*H]
95	        energy = torch.bmm(v, energy)  # [B*1*T]
96	        return energy.squeeze(1)  # [B*T]
97	
98	
99	class Decoder(nn.Module):
100	    """
101	    Apply a gated GRU to decode the input vector.
102	    
103	    Paper/Code Sourced From: https://arxiv.org/pdf/1812.07809.pdf.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, hidden_size, output_size,
107	                 n_layers=1, dropout=0.2):
108	        """Initialize Decoder Mechanism for MCTN.
109	
110	        Args:
111	            hidden_size (int): Size of hidden layer.
112	            output_size (int): Size of output layer
113	            n_layers (int, optional): Number of layers in encoder. Defaults to 1.
114	            dropout (float, optional): Dropout percentage. Defaults to 0.2.
115	        
116	        """
117	        super(Decoder, self).__init__()
118	        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
119	        self.output_size = output_size
120	        self.n_layers = n_layers
121	
122	        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout, inplace=True)
123	        self.attention = Attention(hidden_size)
124	        self.gru = nn.GRU(hidden_size + output_size, hidden_size,
125	                          n_layers, dropout=dropout)
126	        self.out = nn.Linear(hidden_size * 2, output_size)
127	
128	    def forward(self, input, last_hidden, encoder_outputs):
129	        """Apply Decoder Mechanism for MCTN.
130	
131	        Args:
132	            input: Input to MCTN Mechanism.
133	            last_hidden: Last hidden layer input.
134	            encoder_outputs: Output of Encoder object.
135	
136	        Returns:
137	            output: Output of this module.
138	
139	        """
140	        # Get the embedding of the current input word (last output word)
141	        embedded = input.unsqueeze(0)  # (1,B,N)
142	        
143	
144	        # Calculate attention weights and apply to encoder outputs
145	        attn_weights = self.attention(last_hidden[-1], encoder_outputs)
146	        context = attn_weights.bmm(encoder_outputs.transpose(0, 1))  # (B,1,N)
147	        context = context.transpose(0, 1)  # (1,B,N)
148	
149	        # Combine embedded input word and attended context, run through RNN
150	        rnn_input = torch.cat([embedded, context], 2)
151	        output, hidden = self.gru(rnn_input, last_hidden)
152	        output = output.squeeze(0)  # (1,B,N) -> (B,N)
153	        context = context.squeeze(0)
154	        output = self.out(torch.cat([output, context], 1))
155	        output = F.log_softmax(output, dim=1)
156	        return output, hidden, attn_weights
157	
158	
159	class Seq2Seq(nn.Module):
160	    """Implements a Seq2Seq Layer for MCTN."""
161	
162	    def __init__(self, encoder, decoder):
163	        """Initialize Seq2Seq Module.
164	
165	        Args:
166	            encoder (nn.Module): Encoder for the Seq2Seq Layer.
167	            decoder (nn.Module): Decoder for the Seq2Seq Layer.
168	        
169	        """
170	        super(Seq2Seq, self).__init__()
171	        self.encoder = encoder
172	        self.decoder = decoder
173	
174	    def forward(self, src, trg, teacher_forcing_ratio=0.5):
175	        """Apply Seq2Seq Module to Input.
176	
177	        Args:
178	            src (torch.Tensor): Seq2Seq Input
179	            trg (torch.Tensor): Seq2Seq Output for Teacher Forcing.
180	            teacher_forcing_ratio (float, optional): Teacher Forcing Ratio. Set to 0 when evaluating. Defaults to 0.5.
181	
182	        Returns:
183	            _type_: _description_
184	        
185	        """
186	        batch_size = src.size(1)
187	        max_len = src.size(0)
188	
189	        output_size = self.decoder.output_size
190	        outputs = torch.zeros(max_len, batch_size, output_size, device=src.device)
191	
192	        encoder_output, hidden = self.encoder(src)
193	        hidden = hidden[:self.decoder.n_layers]
194	
195	        if self.training:
196	            output = torch.zeros_like(trg[0])  # solve the bug of input.size must be equal to input_size
197	        else:
198	            output = torch.zeros_like(src[0])
199	
200	        for t in range(0, max_len):
201	            output, hidden, attn_weights = self.decoder(
202	                output, hidden, encoder_output)
203	            outputs[t] = output
204	
205	            is_teacher = random.random() < teacher_forcing_ratio
206	            if is_teacher:
207	                output = trg[t].detach()
208	
209	        return outputs, encoder_output
210	
211	
212	class MCTN(nn.Module):
213	    """Implements MCTN."""
214	
215	    def __init__(self, seq2seq, regression_encoder, head, p=0.2):
216	        """Initialize MCTN object.
217	
218	        Args:
219	            seq2seq (nn.Module): Seq2Seq module for MCTN.
220	            regression_encoder (nn.Module): Encoder module for MCTN.
221	            head (nn.Module): Head for MCTN.
222	            p (float, optional): Dropout probability. Defaults to 0.2.
223	        """
224	        super(MCTN, self).__init__()
225	        self.seq2seq = seq2seq
226	        # self.regression = nn.GRU(embedd_dim, reg_hidden_dim,
227	        #                   n_layers, dropout=dropout)
228	        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(p)
229	        self.regression = regression_encoder
230	        self.head = head
231	
232	    def forward(self, src, trg=None):
233	        """Apply Cyclic Joint Embedding in MCTN.
234	
235	        Args:
236	            src (torch.Tensor): Input Tensor
237	            trg (torch.Tensor, optional): Output Tensor for Teacher-Forcing. Defaults to None.
238	
239	        Returns:
240	            torch.Tensor: Output after applying MCTN.
241	        """
242	        # get the cyclic joint embedding!
243	        reout = None
244	        
245	        if self.training:
246	            out, _ = self.seq2seq(src, trg)
247	            
248	            reout, joint_embbed = self.seq2seq(out, src)
249	        else:
250	            # Set teacher_forcing_ratio to zero to get rid of the input of target during inference stage
251	            out, _ = self.seq2seq(src, trg, teacher_forcing_ratio=0.0)
252	            joint_embbed, _ = self.seq2seq.encoder(out)
253	        _, reg = self.regression(joint_embbed)
254	        
255	        reg = self.dropout(reg)
256	        head_out = self.head(reg)[0]
257	        head_out = self.dropout(head_out)
258	        return out, reout, head_out
259	
260	
261	class L2_MCTN(nn.Module):
262	    """Implements 2-Output MCTN."""
263	
264	    def __init__(self, seq2seq_0, seq2seq_1, regression_encoder, head, p=0.2):
265	        """Initialize L2_MCTN.
266	
267	        Args:
268	            seq2seq_0 (nn.Module): Seq2Seq Module converting input to target 1.
269	            seq2seq_1 (nn.Module): Seq2Seq Module converting input to target 2.
270	            regression_encoder (nn.Module): Encoder applied to joint embedding.
271	            head (nn.Module): Head module.
272	            p (float, optional): Dropout percentage. Defaults to 0.2.
273	        
274	        """
275	        super(L2_MCTN, self).__init__()
276	        self.seq2seq0 = seq2seq_0
277	        self.seq2seq1 = seq2seq_1
278	
279	        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(p)
280	        self.regression = regression_encoder
281	        self.head = head
282	
283	    def forward(self, src, trg0=None, trg1=None):
284	        """Apply L2_MCTN to input.
285	
286	        Args:
287	            src (torch.Tensor): Input tensor.
288	            trg0 (torch.Tensor, optional): Target output for Seq2Seq Module 1 Teacher Forcing. Defaults to None.
289	            trg1 (torch.Tensor, optional): Target output for Seq2Seq Module 2 Teacher Forcing. Defaults to None.
290	
291	        Returns:
292	            torch.Tensor: Output for L2_MCTN instance.
293	        """
294	        reout = None
295	        rereout = None
296	        if self.training:
297	            out, _ = self.seq2seq0(src, trg0)
298	            
299	            reout, joint_embbed0 = self.seq2seq0(out, src)
300	            
301	            
302	            rereout, joint_embbed1 = self.seq2seq1(joint_embbed0, trg1)
303	        else:

305	            _, joint_embbed0 = self.seq2seq0.encoder(out)
306	            _, joint_embbed1 = self.seq2seq1.encoder(joint_embbed0)
307	        _, reg = self.regression(joint_embbed1)
308	        reg = self.dropout(reg)
309	        head_out = self.head(reg)[0]
310	        head_out = self.dropout(head_out)
311	        return out, reout, rereout, head_out
312

```text
1	"""
2	Implements fusion network structure for MFAS.
3	
4	See https://github.com/slyviacassell/_MFAS/tree/master/models for hyperparameter details.
5	"""
6	import torch
7	import copy
8	import torch.optim as op
9	from torch import nn
10	import utils.aux_models as aux
11	import utils.scheduler as sc
12	
13	
14	
15	
16	def train_sampled_models(sampled_configurations, searchable_type, dataloaders,
17	                         use_weightsharing, device, unimodal_files, rep_size, classes, sub_sizes, batch_size, epochs,
18	                         eta_max, eta_min, Ti, Tm,
19	                         return_model=False, premodels=False, preaccuracies=False,
20	                         train_only_central_params=True,
21	                         state_dict=dict()):
22	    """Train sampled configurations from MFAS.
23	
24	    Args:
25	        sampled_configurations (List[config]): List of configurations to train on.
26	        searchable_type (rn): Function to create full model from configurations.
27	        dataloaders (List[torch.util.data.DataLoaders]): List of dataloaders to train on.
28	        use_weightsharing (bool): Whether to use weightsharing or not.
29	        device (torch.device): Device to train on.
30	        unimodal_files (List[path]): List of unimodal encoder paths to train on.
31	        rep_size (int): Internal Representation Size
32	        classes (int): Number of classes
33	        sub_sizes (int): Sub sizes.
34	        batch_size (int): Batch size to train on.
35	        epochs (int): Number of epochs to train on.
36	        eta_max (float): Minimum eta of LRCosineAnnealingScheduler
37	        eta_min (float): Maximum eta of LRCosineAnnealingScheduler
38	        Ti (float): Ti for LRCosineAnnealingScheduler
39	        Tm (float): Tm for LRCosineAnnealingScheduler
40	        return_model (bool, optional): Whether to return the trained module as nn.Module. Defaults to False.
41	        premodels (bool, optional): Whether there are pre-trained unimodal models or not. Defaults to False.
42	        preaccuracies (bool, optional): (Unused). Defaults to False.
43	        train_only_central_params (bool, optional): Whether to train only central parameters or not. Defaults to True.
44	        state_dict (_type_, optional): (unused). Defaults to dict().
45	
46	    Returns:
47	        List: List of model accuracies.
48	    """
49	    dataset_sizes = {x: len(dataloaders[x].dataset) for x in ['train', 'dev']}
50	    num_batches_per_epoch = dataset_sizes['train'] / batch_size
51	    criterion = torch.nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
52	
53	    real_accuracies = []
54	
55	    if return_model:
56	        models = []
57	
58	    for idx, configuration in enumerate(sampled_configurations):
59	
60	        if not return_model or idx in return_model:
61	
62	            # model to train
63	            if not premodels:
64	                sds = []
65	                for i in unimodal_files:
66	                    sds.append(torch.load(i, map_location=torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"), weights_only=False))
67	                for sd in sds:
68	                    sd.output_each_layer = True
69	                rmode = searchable_type(
70	                    sds, rep_size, classes, configuration, sub_sizes)
71	
72	            if train_only_central_params:
73	                params = rmode.central_params()
74	
75	            # optimizer and scheduler
76	            optimizer = op.Adam(params, lr=eta_max, weight_decay=1e-4)
77	            scheduler = sc.LRCosineAnnealingScheduler(eta_max, eta_min, Ti, Tm,
78	                                                      num_batches_per_epoch)
79	
80	            rmode.to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
81	
82	            best_model_acc = train_track_acc(rmode, [criterion], optimizer, scheduler, dataloaders,
83	                                             dataset_sizes,
84	                                             device=torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"), num_epochs=epochs, verbose=False,
85	                                             multitask=False)
86	
87	            real_accuracies.append(best_model_acc)
88	
89	            if return_model:
90	                models.append(rmode)
91	
92	    if return_model:
93	        return real_accuracies, models
94	    else:
95	        return real_accuracies
96	
97	
98	def train_track_acc(model, criteria, optimizer, scheduler, dataloaders, dataset_sizes,
99	                    device=None, num_epochs=200, verbose=False, multitask=False):
100	    """Get best accuracy for model when training on a set of dataloaders.
101	
102	    Args:
103	        model (nn.Module): Model to train on.
104	        criteria (nn.Module): Loss function.
105	        optimizer (nn.optim.Optimizer): Optimizer instance
106	        scheduler (nn.optim.Scheduler): LRScheduler to use.
107	        dataloaders (List): List of dataloaders to train on.
108	        dataset_sizes (List): List of the sizes of the datasets
109	        device (torch.device, optional): Device to train on. Defaults to None.
110	        num_epochs (int, optional): Number of epochs to train on. Defaults to 200.
111	        verbose (bool, optional): (Unused) Defaults to False.
112	        multitask (bool, optional): Whether to train as a multitask setting. Defaults to False.
113	
114	    Returns:
115	        float: Best accuracy when training.
116	    """
117	    best_model_sd = copy.deepcopy(model.state_dict())
118	    best_acc = 0
119	
120	    for epoch in range(num_epochs):
121	        # Each epoch has a training and validation phase
122	        for phase in ['train', 'dev']:
123	
124	            if phase == 'train':
125	                if not isinstance(scheduler, sc.LRCosineAnnealingScheduler):
126	                    scheduler.step()
127	                model.train(True)  # Set model to training mode
128	            else:
129	                model.train(False)  # Set model to evaluate mode
130	
131	            running_loss = 0.0
132	            running_corrects = 0
133	
134	            # Iterate over data.
135	            for data in dataloaders[phase]:
136	
137	                # get the inputs
138	                inputs = [d.float().to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")) for d in data[:-1]]
139	                label = data[-1].to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
140	
141	                # zero the parameter gradients
142	                optimizer.zero_grad()
143	
144	                # forward
145	                # track history if only in train
146	                with torch.set_grad_enabled(phase == 'train'):
147	                    output = model(inputs)
148	
149	                    if not multitask:
150	                        _, preds = torch.max(output.detach(), 1)
151	                        loss = criteria[0](output, label)
152	                    else:
153	                        _, preds = torch.max(sum(output), 1)
154	                        loss = criteria[0](output[0], label) + criteria[1](output[1], label) + criteria[2](output[2],
155	                                                                                                           label)
156	
157	                    # backward + optimize only if in training phase
158	                    if phase == 'train':
159	                        if isinstance(scheduler, sc.LRCosineAnnealingScheduler):
160	                            scheduler.step()
161	                            scheduler.update_optimizer(optimizer)
162	                        loss.backward()
163	                        optimizer.step()
164	
165	                # statistics
166	                running_loss += loss.item() * data[0].size(0)
167	                running_corrects += torch.sum(preds == label.detach())
168	
169	            epoch_acc = torch.true_divide(
170	                running_corrects, dataset_sizes[phase])
171	
172	            print('{} Acc: {:.4f}'.format(phase, epoch_acc))
173	
174	            # deep copy the model
175	            if phase == 'dev' and epoch_acc > best_acc:
176	                best_acc = epoch_acc
177	                best_model_sd = copy.deepcopy(model.state_dict())
178	
179	    model.load_state_dict(best_model_sd)
180	    model.train(False)
181	    torch.save(model, 'tests/best'+str(best_acc)+'.pt')
182	
183	    return best_acc
184	
185	
186	class Searchable(nn.Module):
187	    """Implements MFAS's Searchable fusion module."""
188	    
189	    def __init__(self, layered_encoders, rep_size, classes, conf, sub_sizes, alphas=False):
190	        """Instantiate Searchable Module.
191	
192	        Args:
193	            layered_encoders (List): List of nn.Modules for each encoder
194	            rep_size (int): Representation size from unimodals
195	            classes (int): Number of classes
196	            conf (Config): Config instance that generates the layer in question.
197	            sub_sizes (int): Sub sizes
198	            alphas (bool, optional): Whether to generate alphas. Defaults to False.
199	        """
200	        super(Searchable, self).__init__()
201	        self.encoders = nn.ModuleList(layered_encoders)
202	        self.using_alphas = alphas
203	        self.conf = conf
204	        self.subs = sub_sizes
205	        self.hidden = rep_size
206	        self.classes = classes
207	        if alphas:
208	            self.alphas = self.alphasgen()
209	        self.fusion_layers = self.fcs()
210	        self.head = nn.Linear(rep_size, classes)
211	        for m in self.modules():
212	            if isinstance(m, aux.AlphaScalarMultiplication):
213	                nn.init.normal_(m.alpha_x, 0.0, 0.1)
214	
215	    def forward(self, inputs):
216	        """Apply Searchable Module to Layer Inputs.
217	
218	        Args:
219	            inputs (torch.Tensor): List of input tensors
220	
221	        Returns:
222	            torch.Tensor: Layer Output
223	        """
224	        features = []
225	        for i in range(len(inputs)):
226	            feat = self.encoders[i](inputs[i])[1:]
227	            features.append([feat[idx] for idx in self.conf[:, i]])
228	
229	        for layer, conf in enumerate(self.conf):
230	            feats = [f[layer] for f in features]
231	            if self.using_alphas:
232	                aout = self.alphas[layer](feats)
233	            else:
234	                aout = feats
235	            if layer == 0:
236	                fused = torch.cat(aout, 1)
237	                
238	                out = self.fusion_layers[layer](fused)
239	            else:
240	                aout.append(out)
241	                fused = torch.cat(aout, 1)
242	                out = self.fusion_layers[layer](fused)
243	        out = self.head(out)
244	        return out
245	
246	    def central_params(self):
247	        """Define parameters for central module."""
248	        if self.using_alphas:
249	            cent = [{'params': self.alphas.parameters()}, {'params': self.fusion_layers.parameters()}, {
250	                'params': self.head.parameters()}]
251	        else:
252	            cent = [{'params': self.fusion_layers.parameters()}, {
253	                'params': self.head.parameters()}]
254	        return cent
255	
256	    def fcs(self):
257	        """Create fullyconnected layers given config."""
258	        fusion_layers = []
259	        for i, conf in enumerate(self.conf):
260	            in_size = 0
261	            for j in range(len(self.encoders)):
262	                in_size += self.subs[j][self.conf[i][j]]
263	            if i > 0:
264	                in_size += self.hidden
265	            if conf[-1] == 0:
266	                nl = nn.ReLU()
267	            elif conf[-1] == 1:
268	                nl = nn.Sigmoid()
269	            elif conf[-1] == 2:
270	                nl = nn.LeakyReLU()
271	            op = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(in_size, self.hidden), nl)
272	            fusion_layers.append(op)
273	        return nn.ModuleList(fusion_layers)
274	
275	    def alphasgen():
276	        """Generate alpha-layers if stated to do so."""
277	        alphas = [aux.AlphaScalarMultiplication(
278	            self.subs[0][conf[0]], self.subs[0][conf[1]]) for conf in self.conf]
279	        return nn.ModuleList(alphas)
280	
281	
282	def get_possible_layer_configurations(max_labels):
283	    """Generate possible layer configurations.
284	
285	    Args:
286	        max_labels (int): Maximum number of labels
287	
288	    Returns:
289	        list: List of Configuration Instances.
290	    """
291	    list_conf = []
292	    if len(max_labels) == 1:
293	        for a in range(max_labels[0]):
294	            list_conf.append([a])
295	    else:
296	        b = max_labels[1:]
297	        for a in range(max_labels[0]):
298	            li = get_possible_layer_configurations(b)
299	            for k in li:
300	                k.insert(0, a)
301	            list_conf.extend(li)
302	    return list_conf
303
```

1	"""Implements sensor fusion networks for supervised and self-supervised objectives."""
2	import torch
3	import torch.nn as nn
4	from torch.distributions import Normal
5	
6	
7	class SensorFusion(nn.Module): #pragma: no cover
8	    """
9	    Implements traditional SensorFusionNetwork.
10	    
11	    #
12	        Regular SensorFusionNetwork Architecture
13	        Number of parameters:
14	        Inputs:
15	            image:   batch_size x 3 x 128 x 128
16	            force:   batch_size x 6 x 32
17	            proprio: batch_size x 8
18	            action:  batch_size x action_dim
19	    """
20	    
21	    def __init__(
22	        self, device, z_dim=128, action_dim=4, encoder=False, deterministic=False
23	    ):
24	        """Initialize SensorFusionNetwork.
25	
26	        Args:
27	            device (torch.device): Device to train/test model on.
28	            z_dim (int, optional): Z dimension size. Defaults to 128.
29	            action_dim (int, optional): Action dimension size. Defaults to 4.
30	            encoder (bool, optional): Whether to apply action encoder or not. Defaults to False.
31	            deterministic (bool, optional): Whether the fusion networks is deterministic or not. Defaults to False.
32	        """
33	        super().__init__()
34	
35	        self.z_dim = z_dim
36	        self.encoder_bool = encoder
37	        self.device = device
38	        self.deterministic = deterministic
39	
40	        # zero centered, 1 std normal distribution
41	        self.z_prior_m = torch.nn.Parameter(
42	            torch.zeros(1, self.z_dim), requires_grad=False
43	        )
44	        self.z_prior_v = torch.nn.Parameter(
45	            torch.ones(1, self.z_dim), requires_grad=False
46	        )
47	        self.z_prior = (self.z_prior_m, self.z_prior_v)
48	
49	        # -----------------------
50	        # action fusion network
51	        # -----------------------
52	        self.st_fusion_fc1 = nn.Sequential(
53	            nn.Linear(32 + self.z_dim, 128), nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True)
54	        )
55	        self.st_fusion_fc2 = nn.Sequential(
56	            nn.Linear(128, self.z_dim), nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True)
57	        )
58	
59	        if deterministic:
60	            # -----------------------
61	            # modality fusion network
62	            # -----------------------
63	            # 4 Total modalities each (2 * z_dim)
64	            self.fusion_fc1 = nn.Sequential(
65	                nn.Linear(4 * 2 * self.z_dim,
66	                          128), nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True)
67	            )
68	            self.fusion_fc2 = nn.Sequential(
69	                nn.Linear(self.z_dim, self.z_dim), nn.LeakyReLU(
70	                    0.1, inplace=True)
71	            )
72	
73	        # -----------------------
74	        # weight initialization
75	        # -----------------------
76	        for m in self.modules():
77	            if isinstance(m, nn.Conv2d) or isinstance(m, nn.ConvTranspose2d):
78	                nn.init.kaiming_normal_(m.weight.data)
79	                if m.bias is not None:
80	                    m.bias.data.zero_()
81	            elif isinstance(m, nn.BatchNorm2d):
82	                m.weight.data.fill_(1)
83	                m.bias.data.zero_()
84	
85	    def forward_encoder(self, img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded):
86	        """Encode output using forward encoder.
87	
88	        Args:
89	            img_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded image.
90	            frc_encoded (torch.Tesnor): Encoded force.
91	            proprio_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded proprioception
92	            depth_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded depth.
93	            action_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded action.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            torch.Tensor: tuple of outputs
97	        """
98	        # Get encoded outputs
99	        img_out, img_out_convs = img_encoded
100	        depth_out, depth_out_convs = depth_encoded
101	        frc_out = frc_encoded
102	        proprio_out = proprio_encoded
103	
104	        # batch size
105	        batch_dim = img_out.size()[0]
106	
107	        if self.deterministic:
108	            # multimodal embedding
109	            mm_f1 = torch.cat(
110	                [img_out, frc_out, proprio_out, depth_out], 1).squeeze()
111	            mm_f2 = self.fusion_fc1(mm_f1)
112	            z = self.fusion_fc2(mm_f2)
113	
114	        else:
115	            # Encoder priors
116	            mu_prior, var_prior = self.z_prior
117	
118	            # Duplicate prior parameters for each data point in the batch
119	            mu_prior_resized = duplicate(mu_prior, batch_dim).unsqueeze(2)
120	            var_prior_resized = duplicate(var_prior, batch_dim).unsqueeze(2)
121	
122	            # Modality Mean and Variances
123	            mu_z_img, var_z_img = gaussian_parameters(img_out, dim=1)
124	            mu_z_frc, var_z_frc = gaussian_parameters(frc_out, dim=1)
125	            mu_z_proprio, var_z_proprio = gaussian_parameters(
126	                proprio_out, dim=1)
127	            mu_z_depth, var_z_depth = gaussian_parameters(depth_out, dim=1)
128	
129	            # Tile distribution parameters using concatonation
130	            m_vect = torch.cat(
131	                [mu_z_img, mu_z_frc, mu_z_proprio,
132	                    mu_z_depth, mu_prior_resized], dim=2
133	            )
134	            var_vect = torch.cat(
135	                [var_z_img, var_z_frc, var_z_proprio,
136	                    var_z_depth, var_prior_resized],
137	                dim=2,
138	            )
139	
140	            # Fuse modalities mean / variances using product of experts
141	            mu_z, var_z = product_of_experts(m_vect, var_vect)
142	
143	            # Sample Gaussian to get latent
144	            z = sample_gaussian(mu_z, var_z, self.device)
145	
146	        if self.encoder_bool or action_encoded is None:
147	            if self.deterministic:
148	                return img_out, frc_out, proprio_out, depth_out, z
149	            else:
150	                return img_out_convs, img_out, frc_out, proprio_out, depth_out, z
151	        else:
152	            # action embedding
153	            act_feat = action_encoded
154	
155	            # state-action feature
156	            mm_act_f1 = torch.cat([z, act_feat], 1)
157	            mm_act_f2 = self.st_fusion_fc1(mm_act_f1)
158	            mm_act_feat = self.st_fusion_fc2(mm_act_f2)
159	
160	            if self.deterministic:
161	                return img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z
162	            else:
163	                return img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z, mu_z, var_z, mu_prior, var_prior
164	
165	    def weight_parameters(self):
166	        """Get weight parameters."""
167	        return [param for name, param in self.named_parameters() if "weight" in name]
168	
169	    def bias_parameters(self):
170	        """Get bias parameters."""
171	        return [param for name, param in self.named_parameters() if "bias" in name]
172	
173	
174	class SensorFusionSelfSupervised(SensorFusion):
175	    """
176	    Implements SensorFusionNetwork for Self-Supervision.
177	    
178	        Regular SensorFusionNetwork Architecture
179	        Inputs:
180	            image:   batch_size x 3 x 128 x 128
181	            force:   batch_size x 6 x 32
182	            proprio: batch_size x 8
183	            action:  batch_size x action_dim
184	    """
185	
186	    def __init__(
187	        self, device, z_dim=128, encoder=False, deterministic=False
188	    ):
189	        """Initialize SensorFusionSelfSupervised Module.
190	
191	        Args:
192	            device (torch.Device): Device to train/test on.
193	            z_dim (int, optional): Z dimension size for encoders. Defaults to 128.
194	            encoder (bool, optional): Whether to apply the encoders or not. Defaults to False.
195	            deterministic (bool, optional): Whether the fusion network is deterministic or not. Defaults to False.
196	        """
197	        super().__init__(device, z_dim, encoder, deterministic)
198	
199	        self.deterministic = deterministic
200	
201	    def forward(self, input):
202	        """Apply SensorFusionSelfSupervised Module to Layer Input.
203	
204	        Args:
205	            input (torch.Tensor): Layer Input
206	
207	        Returns:
208	            torch.Tensor: Layer Output
209	        """
210	        img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded = input
211	
212	        if self.encoder_bool:
213	            # returning latent space representation if model is set in encoder mode
214	            z = self.forward_encoder(
215	                img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded)
216	            return z
217	
218	        elif action_encoded is None:
219	            z = self.forward_encoder(
220	                img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, None)
221	            pair_out = self.pair_fc(z)
222	            return pair_out
223	
224	        else:
225	            if self.deterministic:
226	                img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z = self.forward_encoder(
227	                    img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded
228	                )
229	            else:
230	                img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z, mu_z, var_z, mu_prior, var_prior = self.forward_encoder(
231	                    img_encoded,
232	                    frc_encoded,
233	                    proprio_encoded,
234	                    depth_encoded,
235	                    action_encoded,
236	                )
237	
238	        # ---------------- Training Objectives ----------------
239	
240	        # tile state-action features and append to conv map
241	        batch_dim = mm_act_feat.size(0)  # batch size
242	        tiled_feat = mm_act_feat.view(
243	            batch_dim, self.z_dim, 1, 1).expand(-1, -1, 2, 2)
244	
245	        if self.deterministic:
246	            return z, mm_act_feat, tiled_feat, img_out_convs
247	        else:
248	            return z, mm_act_feat, tiled_feat, img_out_convs, mu_z, var_z, mu_prior, var_prior
249	
250	
251	class roboticsConcat(nn.Module):
252	    """Concatenates tensors for robotics fusion."""
253	    
254	    def __init__(self, name=None):
255	        """Initialize roboticsConcat module.
256	
257	        Args:
258	            name (str, optional): What kind of concatenation to do. Can be "noconcat", "image" or "simple". Defaults to None.
259	        """
260	        super(roboticsConcat, self).__init__()
261	        self.name = name
262	
263	    def forward(self, x):
264	        """Apply roboticsConcat module to Layer Input.
265	
266	        Args:
267	            x (torch.Tensor): Layer Input
268	
269	        Returns:
270	            torch.Tensor: Layer output.
271	        """
272	        if self.name == "noconcat":
273	            return [x[0][0].squeeze(), x[1].squeeze(), x[2].squeeze(), x[3][0].squeeze(), x[4]]
274	        if self.name == "image":
275	            return torch.cat([x[0][0].squeeze(), x[1][0].squeeze(), x[2]], 1)
276	        if self.name == "simple":
277	            return torch.cat([x[0].squeeze(), x[1]], 1)
278	        return torch.cat([x[0][0].squeeze(), x[1].squeeze(), x[2].squeeze(), x[3][0].squeeze(), x[4]], 1)
279	    
280	
281	def sample_gaussian(m, v, device):
282	    """Sample from Gaussian Distribution with known mean and variance.
283	
284	    Args:
285	        m (float): Mean of sampled Gaussian
286	        v (float): Variance of sampled Gaussian
287	        device (torch.device): Device on which to create tensor.
288	
289	    Returns:
290	        torch.Tensor: Sampled Gaussian Tensor
291	    """
292	    epsilon = Normal(0, 1).sample(m.size())
293	    z = m + torch.sqrt(v) * epsilon.to(device)
294	
295	    return z
296	
297	
298	def gaussian_parameters(h, dim=-1):
299	    """Return parameters for gaussian distribution given vector for robotics."""
300	    m, h = torch.split(h, h.size(dim) // 2, dim=dim)
301	    v = F.softplus(h) + 1e-8
302	    return m, v
303	
304	
305	def product_of_experts(m_vect, v_vect):
306	    """Compute product of experts."""
307	    T_vect = 1.0 / v_vect
308	
309	    mu = (m_vect * T_vect).sum(2) * (1 / T_vect.sum(2))
310	    var = 1 / T_vect.sum(2)
311	
312	    return mu, var
313	
314	
315	def duplicate(x, rep):
316	    """Duplicate x rep times.
317	
318	    Args:
319	        x (torch.Tensor): Tensor to replicate
320	        rep (int): Number of times to replicate
321	
322	    Returns:
323	        torch.Tensor: Replicated Tensor
324	    """
325	    return x.expand(rep, *x.shape).reshape(-1, *x.shape[1:])
326	
327	
328	def depth_deconv(in_planes, out_planes):
329	    """Instantiate depth deconvolution module.
330	
331	    Args:
332	        in_planes (int): Number of input channels
333	        out_planes (int): Number of output channels
334	
335	    Returns:
336	        nn.Module: Depth Deconvolution Module
337	    """
338	    return nn.Sequential(
339	        nn.Conv2d(
340	            in_planes, 16, kernel_size=3, stride=1, padding=(3 - 1) // 2, bias=True
341	        ),
342	        nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True),
343	        nn.Conv2d(16, 16, kernel_size=3, stride=1,
344	                  padding=(3 - 1) // 2, bias=True),
345	        nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True),
346	        nn.ConvTranspose2d(
347	            16, out_planes, kernel_size=4, stride=2, padding=1, bias=True
348	        ),
349	        nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True),
350	    )
351	
352

```text
1	"""Implements training structures for gradient blending."""
2	import sklearn.metrics
3	import torch
4	from torch import nn
5	import copy
6	from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Subset
7	from eval_scripts.performance import AUPRC, f1_score, accuracy
8	from eval_scripts.complexity import all_in_one_train, all_in_one_test
9	from eval_scripts.robustness import relative_robustness, effective_robustness, single_plot
10	from tqdm import tqdm
11	
12	criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
13	delta = False
14	
15	
16	def getloss(model, head, data, monum, batch_size):
17	    """Get loss for model given classification head.
18	
19	    Args:
20	        model (nn.Module): Module to evaluate
21	        head (nn.Module): Classification head.
22	        data (torch.utils.data.Dataloader): Dataloader to evaluate on.
23	        monum (int): Unimodal model index.
24	        batch_size (int): (unused) Batch Size
25	
26	    Returns:
27	        float: Average loss per sample.
28	    """
29	    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
30	    losses = 0.0
31	    total = 0
32	    with torch.inference_mode():
33	        for j in data:
34	            total += len(j[0])
35	            train_x = j[monum].float().to(device)
36	            train_y = j[-1].to(device)
37	            out = model(train_x)
38	            # if (monum==1):
39	            
40	            out = head(out)
41	            loss = criterion(out, train_y.squeeze())
42	            
43	            losses += loss*len(j[0])
44	    return losses/total
45	
46	
47	def train_unimodal(model, head, optim, trains, valids, monum, epoch, batch_size):
48	    """Train unimodal gradient blending module.
49	
50	    Args:
51	        model (nn.Module): Unimodal encoder
52	        head (nn.Module): Classifier instance
53	        optim (torch.optim.Optimizer): Optimizer instance
54	        trains (torch.utils.data.DataLoader):  Training Dataloader Instance
55	        valids (torch.utils.data.DataLoader):  Validation DataLoader Instance
56	        monum (int): Modality index
57	        epoch (int): Number of epochs to train on
58	        batch_size (int): Batch size of data loaders
59	
60	    Returns:
61	        float: Metric
62	    """
63	    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
64	    ltN = getloss(model, head, trains, monum, batch_size)
65	    lvN = getloss(model, head, valids, monum, batch_size)
66	    for i in range(epoch):
67	        totalloss = 0.0
68	        total = 0
69	        for j in trains:
70	            total += len(j[0])
71	            train_x = j[monum].float().to(device)
72	            train_y = j[-1].to(device)
73	            optim.zero_grad()
74	            out = model(train_x)
75	            out = head(out)
76	            loss = criterion(out, train_y.squeeze())
77	            totalloss += loss.item() * len(j[0])
78	            loss.backward()
79	            optim.step()
80	        print("Epoch "+str(i)+" loss: "+str(totalloss / total))
81	
82	    ltNn = getloss(model, head, trains, monum, batch_size)
83	    lvNn = getloss(model, head, valids, monum, batch_size)
84	    print("Final train loss: "+str(ltNn)+" valid loss: "+str(lvNn))
85	    oNn = lvNn-ltNn
86	    oN = lvN-ltN
87	    if delta:
88	        oi = oNn-oN
89	        g = lvNn-lvN
90	    else:
91	        oi = oNn
92	        if oi < 0:
93	            oi = 0.0001
94	        g = lvNn
95	    print("raw: "+str(g/(oi*oi)))
96	    return abs(g/(oi*oi))
97	
98	
99	def multimodalcondense(models, fuse, train_x):
100	    """Compute fusion encoded output.
101	
102	    Args:
103	        models (List): List of nn.Modules for each encoder
104	        fuse (nn.Module): Fusion instance
105	        train_x (List): List of Input Tensors
106	
107	    Returns:
108	        torch.Tensor: Fused output
109	    """
110	    outs = multimodalcompute(models, train_x)
111	    return fuse(outs)
112	
113	
114	def multimodalcompute(models, train_x):
115	    """Compute encoded representation for each modality in train_x using encoders in models.
116	
117	    Args:
118	        models (list): List of encoder instances
119	        train_x (List): List of Input Tensors
120	
121	    Returns:
122	        List: List of encoded tensors
123	    """
124	    outs = []
125	    for i in range(len(models)):
126	        outs.append(models[i](train_x[i]))
127	    return outs
128	
129	
130	def getmloss(models, head, fuse, data, batch_size):
131	    """Calculate multimodal loss.
132	
133	    Args:
134	        models (list): List of encoder models
135	        head (nn.Module): Classifier module
136	        fuse (nn.Module): Fusion module
137	        data (torch.utils.data.Dataloader): Data loader to calculate loss on.
138	        batch_size (int): Batch size of dataloader
139	
140	    Returns:
141	        float: Average loss
142	    """
143	    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
144	    losses = 0.0
145	    total = 0
146	    with torch.inference_mode():
147	        for j in data:
148	            total += len(j[0])
149	            train_x = [x.float().to(device) for x in j[:-1]]
150	            train_y = j[-1].to(device)
151	            out = head(multimodalcondense(models, fuse, train_x))
152	            loss = criterion(out, train_y.squeeze())
153	            losses += loss*len(j[0])
154	    return losses/float(total)
155	
156	
157	def train_multimodal(models, head, fuse, optim, trains, valids, epoch, batch_size):
158	    """Train multimodal gradient-blending model.
159	
160	    Args:
161	        models (list): List of nn.modules for the encoders
162	        head (nn.Module): Classifier, post fusion layer
163	        fuse (nn.Module): Fusion module
164	        optim (torch.optim.Optimizer): Optimizer instance.
165	        trains (torch.utils.data.Dataloader): Training data dataloader
166	        valids (torch.utils.data.Dataloader): Validation data dataloader
167	        epoch (int): Number of epochs to train on
168	        batch_size (int): Batch size
169	
170	    Returns:
171	        float: metric
172	    """
173	    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
174	    ltN = getmloss(models, head, fuse, trains, batch_size)
175	    lvN = getmloss(models, head, fuse, valids, batch_size)
176	    for i in range(epoch):
177	        totalloss = 0.0
178	        total = 0
179	        for j in trains:
180	            total += len(j[0])
181	            train_x = [x.float().to(device) for x in j[:-1]]
182	            train_y = j[-1].to(device)
183	            optim.zero_grad()
184	            out = head(multimodalcondense(models, fuse, train_x))
185	            loss = criterion(out, train_y.squeeze())
186	            totalloss += loss.item()*len(j[0])
187	            loss.backward()
188	            optim.step()
189	        print("Epoch "+str(i)+" loss: "+str(totalloss/total))
190	    ltNn = getmloss(models, head, fuse, trains, batch_size)
191	    lvNn = getmloss(models, head, fuse, valids, batch_size)
192	    print("Final train loss: "+str(ltNn)+" valid loss: "+str(lvNn))
193	    oNn = lvNn-ltNn
194	    oN = lvN-ltN
195	    if delta:
196	        oi = oNn-oN
197	        g = lvNn-lvN
198	    else:
199	        oi = oNn
200	        if oi < 0:
201	            oi = 0.0001
202	        g = lvNn
203	    print("raw: "+str(g/(oi*oi)))
204	    return abs(g/(oi*oi))
205	
206	
207	def gb_estimate(unimodal_models, multimodal_classification_head, fuse, unimodal_classification_heads, train_dataloader, gb_epoch,
208	                batch_size, v_dataloader, lr, weight_decay=0.0, optimtype=torch.optim.SGD):
209	    """Compute estimate of gradient-blending score.
210	
211	    Args:
212	        unimodal_models (list): List of encoder modules
213	        multimodal_classification_head (nn.Module): Classifier given fusion instance
214	        fuse (nn.Module): Fusion module
215	        unimodal_classification_heads (list): List of unimodal classifiers
216	        train_dataloader (torch.utils.data.Dataloader): Training data loader
217	        gb_epoch (int): Number of epochs for gradient-blending
218	        batch_size (int): Batch size
219	        v_dataloader (torch.utils.data.Dataloader): Validation dataloader
220	        lr (float): Learning Rate
221	        weight_decay (float, optional): Weight decay parameter. Defaults to 0.0.
222	        optimtype (torch.optim.Optimizer, optional): Optimizer instance. Defaults to torch.optim.SGD.
223	
224	    Returns:
225	        float: Normalized weights between unimodal and multimodal models
226	    """
227	    weights = []
228	    for i in range(len(unimodal_models)):
229	        print("At gb_estimate unimodal "+str(i))
230	        model = copy.deepcopy(unimodal_models[i]).to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
231	        head = copy.deepcopy(unimodal_classification_heads[i]).to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
232	        optim = optimtype(list(model.parameters()) +
233	                          list(head.parameters()), lr=lr, weight_decay=weight_decay)
234	        w = train_unimodal(model, head, optim, train_dataloader,
235	                           v_dataloader, i, gb_epoch, batch_size)
236	        weights.append(w)
237	    print("At gb_estimate multimodal ")
238	    allcopies = [copy.deepcopy(x).to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")) for x in unimodal_models]
239	    mmcopy = copy.deepcopy(multimodal_classification_head).to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
240	    fusecopy = copy.deepcopy(fuse).to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
241	    params = []
242	    for model in allcopies:
243	        params.extend(list(model.parameters()))
244	    params.extend(list(mmcopy.parameters()))
245	    if fusecopy.parameters() is not None:
246	        params.extend(list(fusecopy.parameters()))
247	    optim = optimtype(params, lr=lr, weight_decay=weight_decay)
248	    weights.append(train_multimodal(allcopies, mmcopy, fusecopy,
249	                                    optim, train_dataloader, v_dataloader, gb_epoch, batch_size))
250	    z = sum(weights)
251	    return [(w/z).item() for w in weights]
252	
253	
254	softmax = nn.Softmax()
255	
256	
257	class completeModule(nn.Module):
258	    """Implements and combines sub-modules into a full classifier."""
259	    def __init__(self, encoders, fuse, head):
260	        """Instantiate completeModule instance.
261	
262	        Args:
263	            encoders (list): List of nn.Module encoders
264	            fuse (nn.Module): Fusion module
265	            head (nn.Module): Classifier module
266	        """
267	        super(completeModule, self).__init__()
268	        self.encoders = nn.ModuleList(encoders)
269	        self.fuse = fuse
270	        self.head = head
271	
272	    def forward(self, x):
273	        """Apply classifier to output.
274	
275	        Args:
276	            x (list[torch.Tensor]): List of input tensors
277	
278	        Returns:
279	            torch.Tensor: Classifier output
280	        """
281	        outs = multimodalcondense(self.encoders, self.fuse, x)
282	        return self.head(outs)
283	
284	
285	def calcAUPRC(pts):
286	    """Calculate AUPRC score given true labels and predicted probabilities.
287	
288	    Args:
289	        pts (list): List of (true, predicted prob) for each sample in batch.
290	
291	    Returns:
292	        float: AUPRC score
293	    """
294	    true_labels = [int(x[1]) for x in pts]
295	    predicted_probs = [x[0] for x in pts]
296	    return sklearn.metrics.average_precision_score(true_labels, predicted_probs)
297	
298	
299	
300	
301	def train(unimodal_models,  multimodal_classification_head,
302	          unimodal_classification_heads, fuse, train_dataloader, valid_dataloader,
303	          num_epoch, lr, gb_epoch=20, v_rate=0.08, weight_decay=0.0, optimtype=torch.optim.SGD,
304	          finetune_epoch=25, classification=True, AUPRC=False, savedir='best.pt', track_complexity=True):
305	    """Train model using gradient_blending.
306	
307	    Args:
308	        unimodal_models (list): List of modules, unimodal encoders for each input modality in the order of the modality input data.
309	        multimodal_classification_head (nn.Module): Classification head that takes in fused output of unimodal models of all modalities
310	        unimodal_classification_heads (list[nn.Module]): List of classification heads that each takes in output of one unimodal model (must be in the same modality order as unimodal_models)
311	        fuse (nn.Module): Fusion module that takes in a list of outputs from unimodal_models and generate a fused representation
312	        train_dataloader (torch.utils.data.DataLoader): Training data loader
313	        valid_dataloader (torch.utils.data.DataLoader): Validation data loader
314	        num_epoch (int): Number of epochs to train this model on.
315	        lr (float): Learning rate.
316	        gb_epoch (int, optional): Number of epochs between re-evaluation of weights of gradient blend. Defaults to 20.
317	        v_rate (float, optional): Portion of training set used as validation for gradient blend weight estimation. Defaults to 0.08.
318	        weight_decay (float, optional): Weight decay of optimizer. Defaults to 0.0.
319	        optimtype (torch.optim.Optimizer, optional):  Type of optimizer to use. Defaults to torch.optim.SGD.
320	        finetune_epoch (int, optional): Number of epochs to finetune the classification head. Defaults to 25.
321	        classification (bool, optional): Whether the task is a classification task. Defaults to True.
322	        AUPRC (bool, optional): Whether to compute auprc score or not. Defaults to False.
323	        savedir (str, optional): The name of the saved file for the model with current best validation performance. Defaults to 'best.pt'.
324	        track_complexity (bool, optional): Whether to track complexity or not. Defaults to True.
325	    """
326	    def _trainprocess():
327	        nonlocal train_dataloader
328	        device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
329	        params = []
330	        for model in unimodal_models:
331	            params.extend(model.parameters())
332	        for model in unimodal_classification_heads:
333	            params.extend(model.parameters())
334	        params.extend(multimodal_classification_head.parameters())
335	        params.extend(fuse.parameters())
336	        optim = optimtype(params, lr=lr, weight_decay=weight_decay)
337	        train_datas = train_dataloader.dataset
338	        splitloc = int(len(train_datas)*v_rate)
339	        inds = list(range(len(train_datas)))
340	        train_inds = inds[splitloc:]
341	        v_inds = inds[0:splitloc]
342	        train_data = Subset(train_datas, train_inds)
343	        v_data = Subset(train_datas, v_inds)
344	        train_dataloader = DataLoader(
345	            train_data, shuffle=True, num_workers=8, batch_size=train_dataloader.batch_size)
346	        tv_dataloader = DataLoader(
347	            v_data, shuffle=False, num_workers=8, batch_size=train_dataloader.batch_size)
348	        finetunehead = copy.deepcopy(multimodal_classification_head).to(device)
349	        fusehead = copy.deepcopy(fuse).to(device)
350	        params = list(finetunehead.parameters())
351	        if fuse.parameters() is not None:
352	            params.extend(list(fuse.parameters()))
353	        optimi = optimtype(params, lr=lr, weight_decay=weight_decay)
354	        bestvalloss = 10000.0
355	        for i in range(num_epoch//gb_epoch):
356	            weights = gb_estimate(unimodal_models,  multimodal_classification_head, fuse,
357	                                  unimodal_classification_heads, train_dataloader, gb_epoch, train_dataloader.batch_size, tv_dataloader, lr, weight_decay, optimtype)
358	            print("epoch "+str(i*gb_epoch)+" weights: "+str(weights))
359	            for jj in range(gb_epoch):
360	                totalloss = 0.0
361	                for j in train_dataloader:
362	                    train_x = [x.float().to(device) for x in j[:-1]]
363	                    train_y = j[-1].to(device)
364	                    optim.zero_grad()
365	                    outs = multimodalcompute(unimodal_models, train_x)
366	                    fuse.train()
367	                    multimodal_classification_head.train()
368	                    catout = fuse(outs)
369	                    blendloss = criterion(multimodal_classification_head(
370	                        catout), train_y.squeeze())*weights[-1]
371	                    for ii in range(len(unimodal_models)):
372	                        loss = criterion(unimodal_classification_heads[ii](
373	                            outs[ii]), train_y.squeeze())
374	                        blendloss += loss * weights[ii]
375	                    totalloss += blendloss.item()*len(j[0])
376	                    blendloss.backward()
377	                    optim.step()
378	                print("epoch "+str(jj+i*gb_epoch)+" blend train loss: " +
379	                      str(totalloss/len(train_data)))
380	            # finetunes classification head
381	            finetunetrains = []
382	            with torch.inference_mode():
383	                for j in train_dataloader:
384	                    train_x = [x.float().to(device) for x in j[:-1]]
385	                    train_y = j[-1].to(device)
386	                    outs = multimodalcompute(unimodal_models, train_x)
387	                    for iii in range(len(train_y)):
388	                        aa = [x[iii].cpu() for x in outs]
389	
390	                        aa.append(train_y[iii].cpu())
391	
392	                        finetunetrains.append(aa)
393	            print("Length of ftt_dataloader: "+str(len(finetunetrains)))
394	            ftt_dataloader = DataLoader(
395	                finetunetrains, shuffle=True, num_workers=8, batch_size=train_dataloader.batch_size)
396	            for jj in range(finetune_epoch):
397	                totalloss = 0.0
398	                for j in ftt_dataloader:
399	                    optimi.zero_grad()
400	                    train_x = [x.float().to(device) for x in j[:-1]]
401	                    train_y = j[-1].to(device)
402	                    finetunehead.train()
403	                    fusehead.train()
404	                    blendloss = criterion(finetunehead(
405	                        fusehead(train_x)), train_y.squeeze())
406	                    totalloss += blendloss.item() * len(j[0])
407	                    blendloss.backward()
408	                    optimi.step()
409	                print("finetune train loss: "+str(totalloss/len(train_data)))
410	                with torch.inference_mode():
411	                    totalloss = 0.0
412	                    total = 0
413	                    corrects = 0
414	                    auprclist = []
415	                    for j in valid_dataloader:
416	                        valid_x = [x.float().to(device) for x in j[:-1]]
417	                        valid_y = j[-1].to(device)
418	                        outs = multimodalcompute(unimodal_models, valid_x)
419	                        fusehead.eval()
420	                        catout = fusehead(outs)
421	                        finetunehead.eval()
422	                        predicts = finetunehead(catout)
423	                        blendloss = criterion(predicts, valid_y.squeeze())
424	                        totalloss += blendloss.item()*len(j[0])
425	                        total += len(j[0])
426	                        if AUPRC:
427	                            for ii in range(len(j[0])):
428	                                predictval = softmax(predicts[ii])
429	                                auprclist.append(
430	                                    (predictval[1].item(), valid_y[ii].item()))
431	                        if classification:
432	                            predicted = torch.argmax(predicts, dim=1)
433	                            corrects += (predicted == valid_y.squeeze().long()).sum().item()
434	                    valoss = totalloss/total
435	                    print("epoch "+str((i+1)*gb_epoch-1)+" valid loss: "+str(totalloss/total) +
436	                          ((" acc: "+str(float(corrects)/total)) if classification else ''))
437	                    if AUPRC:
438	                        print("With AUPRC: "+str(calcAUPRC(auprclist)))
439	                    if valoss < bestvalloss:
440	                        bestvalloss = valoss
441	                        print("Saving best")
442	                        torch.save(completeModule(unimodal_models,
443	                                                  fusehead, finetunehead), savedir)
444	    if track_complexity:
445	        all_in_one_train(_trainprocess, unimodal_models +
446	                         [multimodal_classification_head, fuse]+unimodal_classification_heads)
447	    else:
448	        _trainprocess()
449

451	def single_test(model, test_dataloader, auprc=False, classification=True):
452	    """Run single test with model and test data loader.
453	
454	    Args:
455	        model (nn.Module): Model to evaluate.
456	        test_dataloader (torch.utils.data.DataLoader): Test data loader
457	        auprc (bool, optional): Whether to return AUPRC scores or not. Defaults to False.
458	        classification (bool, optional): Whether to return classification accuracy or not. Defaults to True.
459	
460	    Returns:
461	        dict: Dictionary of (metric, value) pairs
462	    """
463	    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
464	    with torch.inference_mode():
465	        totalloss = 0.0
466	        total = 0
467	        corrects = 0
468	        auprclist = []
469	        for j in test_dataloader:
470	            valid_x = [x.float().to(device) for x in j[:-1]]
471	            valid_y = j[-1].to(device)
472	            predicts = model(valid_x)
473	            blendloss = criterion(predicts, valid_y.squeeze())
474	            totalloss += blendloss.item()*len(j[0])
475	            total += len(j[0])
476	            if auprc:
477	                for ii in range(len(j[0])):
478	                    predictval = softmax(predicts[ii])
479	                    auprclist.append(
480	                        (predictval[1].item(), valid_y[ii].item()))
481	            if classification:
482	                predicted = torch.argmax(predicts, dim=1)
483	                corrects += (predicted == valid_y.squeeze().long()).sum().item()
484	        print("test loss: "+str(totalloss/total) +
485	              ((" acc: "+str(float(corrects)/total)) if classification else ''))
486	        if auprc:
487	            print("With AUPRC: "+str(calcAUPRC(auprclist)))
488	    if classification:
489	        return {'Accuracy': float(corrects)/total}
490	    else:
491	        return {'MSE': totalloss/total}
492	
493	
494	def test(model, test_dataloaders_all, dataset, method_name='My method', auprc=False, classification=True, no_robust=False):
495	    """Test module, reporting results to stdout.
496	
497	    Args:
498	        model (nn.Module): Model to test
499	        test_dataloaders_all (list[torch.utils.data.Dataloader]): List of data loaders to test on.
500	        dataset (string): Dataset name
501	        method_name (str, optional): Method name. Defaults to 'My method'.
502	        auprc (bool, optional): Whether to use AUPRC scores or not. Defaults to False.
503	        classification (bool, optional): Whether the task is classificaion or not. Defaults to True.
504	        no_robust (bool, optional): Whether to not apply robustness variations to input. Defaults to False.
505	    """
506	    if no_robust:
507	        def _testprocess():
508	            single_test(model, test_dataloaders_all, auprc, classification)
509	        all_in_one_test(_testprocess, [model])
510	        return
511	
512	    def _testprocess():
513	        single_test(model, test_dataloaders_all[list(
514	            test_dataloaders_all.keys())[0]][0], auprc, classification)
515	    all_in_one_test(_testprocess, [model])
516	    for noisy_modality, test_dataloaders in test_dataloaders_all.items():
517	        print("Testing on noisy data ({})...".format(noisy_modality))
518	        robustness_curve = dict()
519	        for test_dataloader in tqdm(test_dataloaders):
520	            single_test_result = single_test(
521	                model, test_dataloader, auprc, classification)
522	            for k, v in single_test_result.items():
523	                curve = robustness_curve.get(k, [])
524	                curve.append(v)
525	                robustness_curve[k] = curve
526	        for measure, robustness_result in robustness_curve.items():
527	            robustness_key = '{} {}'.format(dataset, noisy_modality)
528	            print("relative robustness ({}, {}): {}".format(noisy_modality, measure, str(
529	                relative_robustness(robustness_result, robustness_key))))
530	            if len(robustness_curve) != 1:
531	                robustness_key = '{} {}'.format(robustness_key, measure)
532	            print("effective robustness ({}, {}): {}".format(noisy_modality, measure, str(
533	                effective_robustness(robustness_result, robustness_key))))
534	            fig_name = '{}-{}-{}-{}'.format(method_name,
535	                                            robustness_key, noisy_modality, measure)
536	            single_plot(robustness_result, robustness_key, xlabel='Noise level',
537	                        ylabel=measure, fig_name=fig_name, method=method_name)
538	            print("Plot saved as "+fig_name)

24	    
25	    See https://github.com/slyviacassell/_MFAS/blob/master/models/searchable.py for more details.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        unimodal_files (list[dict]): Dictionary of names of files containing pretrained unimodal encoders
29	        rep_size (int): Size of Representation
